info.title: Appveyor Cli global tool
info.description:
appvey Works seamlessly with Appveyor CI/CD from the command line, helping users to maintain appveyor projects, run builds and download artifacts using Appveyor REST API.
Signed by signpath.io from repository 'https://github.com/moh-hassan/appvey' commit '4bb877d9d7a7663654bbbc5fd7e5cd73ca36a802' (see contained AppVeyorSettings.json file for build settings).

commands.name: build
commands.description: Build with more nested commands...

commands.name: cancel
commands.description:
Cancel build.
Example:
appvey build cancel -a my-account -t my-token -s my-project 1.2.3

options.name: --token
options.description:
Appveyor token v2.
To enter your token, you have four options:
• Enter your token from your keyboard.
• Type a hyphen ('-'): This allows you to directly type or paste your token from your keyboard.
• Type '@' followed by the filename: This tells the system to read your token from the specified file.
• Ignore this option: If you don't enter anything, the system will try to automatically read your token from an environment variable or Windows Credential Manager: Available for Windows users only. This assumes you have already set up the token using config command.

options.name: --account
options.description: Appveyor User account: Optional. If not provided, the system will look for your account in the environment variables. This assumes you have already set up the account using config command.

options.name: --proxy-address
options.description: Proxy server should be in the form http://proxy:port

options.name: --proxy-user
options.description: Proxy user/password should be in the form 'username:password'

options.name: --what-if
options.description:
Run the command without executing the actions of the command so no changes occur.
It displays optins and argument values and the expected Http Request:
(Url, Method  <Get|Post|Put|Delete>, JsonBody. [default: False]

commands.name: delete
commands.description:
Delete build.
Example:
appvey build delete -a my-account -t my-token 123456

commands.name: download
commands.description: Download with more nested commands...

commands.name: artifacts
commands.description:
Download build artifacts of last build or by a version in the project.
Example:
appvey build download artifacts -a my-account -t my-token -s my-project -d my-directory

options.name: --version
options.description: Build version. Both job-id and version are mutually exclusive, either JobId or Version must be specified

options.name: --dir
options.description: Directory where to save artifacts, relative or absolute path [default: .]

options.name: --token
options.description:
Appveyor token v2.
To enter your token, you have four options:
• Enter your token from your keyboard.
• Type a hyphen ('-'): This allows you to directly type or paste your token from your keyboard.

• Ignore this option: If you don't enter anything, the system will try to automatically read your token from an environment variable or Windows Credential Manager: Available for Windows users only. This assumes you have already set up the token using config command.

commands.name: log
commands.description:
Download build log.
Example 1:
appvey build download log -a my-account -t my-token -s my-project --job-id 1234
Example 2:
appvey build download log -a my-account -t my-token -s my-project --version 1.2.3

options.name: --version
options.description:
Build version. You can only specify one of the following: job-id or build-version.
You cannot use both at the same time.

options.name: --token

commands.name: rerun
commands.description:
Re-run build.
Example:
appvey build rerun -a my-account -t my-token -s my-project 1234

options.name: --incomplete
options.description: False (default value) for full build re-run. Set it to True to rerun only failed or cancelled jobs in multi jobs build. [default: False]

options.name: --slug
options.description: A Slug is not required. However, you might need to navigate to the build page to find the information of the build during run.

commands.name: start
commands.description: Start build with more nested commands...

commands.name: commit
commands.description:
Start build of specific branch commit.
Example:
appvey build start commit -a my-account  -t my-token -s my-project -b master 856cf6c2

options.name: --browse
options.description: Browse to the build page [default: False]

commands.name: pull-request
commands.description:
Start build of Pull Request.
Example :
appvey build start pr -a my-account -t my-token -s my-project 1234

commands.name: recent
commands.description:
Start build of branch most recent commit.
Example 1:
appvey build start recent -s myproject --browse
Example 2 using environment variables with = separator:
appvey build start recent -a my-account -t my-token -s my-project --browse  var1=value1 var2=value2
Example 3 using file:
appvey build start recent -s myproject --browse @myenv.txt

arguments.name: ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLES
arguments.description:
The list of key/value pairs separated by space.
Every variable should be in the form:
<varName:value>
OR <varName=value>
OR use file with @ prefix like <@filename> which contain the Var/Value pairs

commands.name: config
commands.description:
Configure Appveyor token and account and allow storing token to Environment variables
or Windows Credential Manager (Windows users only).
Example 1: Storing token to Environment Variable
appvey config -a my-account -t my-token --action save
Example 2: Storing token to Windows Credential Manager
appvey config -a my-account -t my-token --action save --use-cred
Example 3: Show token store information
appvey config -a my-account --action info

options.name: --use-cred
options.description: Store token in Windows Credential Manager (Windows only). [default: False]

commands.name: http
commands.description:
Run Appveyor Rest Api Get/Post/Put/Delete.
Example 1: Get request
appvey /api/projects/my-account/my-project/history?recordsNumber=20
Example 2: Post request using json file
appvey -m post --json @file.json  /api/account/my-account/builds

options.name: --method
options.description: Http method but Case insensitive [default: Get]

options.name: --token
options.description:
Appveyor token v2.

• Enter your token from your keyboard.
• Type a hyphen ('-'): This allows you to directly type or paste your token from your keyboard.

commands.name: project
commands.description: Project command

commands.name: add
commands.description:
Add project.
Example 1: Add project for github
appvey project add -a my-account -t my-token my-account/my-project
Example 2: Add project for gitlab
appvey project add -a my-account -t my-token -p gitlab my-account/my-project

Example 1:

Example 2:

It displays optins and argument values and the expected Http Request:
(Url, Method  <Get|Post|Put|Delete>, JsonBody. [default: False]

commands.name: update
commands.description: Update with more nested commands...

commands.name: build-number
commands.description:
Update project build number.
Example 1:
appvey project update build-number -a my-account -t my-token -s my-project 30
Example 2:
appvey project update bn -a my-account -t my-token -s my-project 30

options.name: --token
options.description:
Appveyor token v2.
To enter your token, you have four options:
• Enter your token from your keyboard.
• Type a hyphen ('-'): This allows you to directly type or paste your token from your keyboard.
• Type '@' followed by the filename: This tells the system to read your token from the specified file.
• Ignore this option: If you don't enter anything, the system will try to automatically read your token from an environment variable or Windows Credential Manager: Available for Windows users only. This assumes you have already set up the token using config command.

options.name: --account
options.description: Appveyor User account: Optional. If not provided, the system will look for your account in the environment variables. This assumes you have already set up the account using config command.

options.name: --proxy-address
options.description: Proxy server should be in the form http://proxy:port

options.name: --proxy-user
options.description: Proxy user/password should be in the form 'username:password'

options.name: --what-if
options.description:
Run the command without executing the actions of the command so no changes occur.
It displays optins and argument values and the expected Http Request:
(Url, Method  <Get|Post|Put|Delete>, JsonBody. [default: False]

commands.name: env
commands.description:
Update project environment variables.
Example:
appvey project update env -a my-account -t my-token -s my-project var1:value1  secret_var:123ab45:true

options.name: --json
options.description: File name that contain environment json string. Only Env arguments or Json option is allowed

arguments.name: ENV
arguments.description:
Environment variables are defined in the format 'name:value'.
If the value is encrypted, please include 'true' after the value (e.g., 'name:value:true').

commands.name: repo
commands.description:
Update Appveyor project slug.
Example:
appvey project update slug -a my-account -t my-token -j file.json
